strategy: Clear its trap cheat before killing it. Termonarch stacks a chromatic Keeper of the Flame trap on your highest-Health wizard every round, and if it dies while that cheat is active it revives at full Health.
Use the intended counter: a Life damage spell that removes traps from wizards (like Leprechaun Tier 2b) strips all those traps and ends the Keeper of the Flame cheat, disabling the revive.
Line the kill up right after you clear. Cast the trap-removing Life spell, then finish Termonarch the same window so the cheat cannot re-arm before the kill.
It is Myth and takes boosted Fire and Life, so a Fire or Life hitter fits; the Leprechaun-style Life counter doubles as your setup clear.
It has 6 starting pips and only 4,040 Health, so it can hit early but dies fast once the revive is disabled.
Take the free Centaur card it hands out at the end of Round 4 (and on each revive) if useful, then burst it down with the Keeper of the Flame cheat cleared.
cheatNotes: Every round it casts a chromatic trap (Keeper of the Flame) on your highest-Health wizard, starting +15% and rising 5% per round to a 40% cap.
If Termonarch dies while the Keeper of the Flame cheat is still active, it revives with full Health, so clear the trap cheat before the kill.
At the end of Round 4 and whenever it revives, it gives all wizards a free Centaur (Tier 2b) card.
Casting a Life damage spell that removes traps from wizards, such as Leprechaun (Tier 2b), strips all those traps and ends the Keeper of the Flame cheat, disabling the revive.
